title: halimium lasianthum subsp. formosum
intro: Halimium lasianthum subsp. formosum, also known as a type of rockrose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Cistaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and shrub-like growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Halimium lasianthum subsp. formosum are believed to include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves or flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves for topical use.
